summary: Backend + frontend testing for ASN Manual Batch Allocation. Backend: 16/16 pytest cases pass (multi-batch happy path with pause + confirm/validate/persist + master_dispatch link + export column + workflow drill-down; BATCH-LOW cancel path -> Failed with 'cancelled by user' + after_failure screenshot + NO Retry event; non-BATCH regression completes without pause; validation 400s for over-available, unknown batch, total mismatch; 409 for confirm/cancel when no pending allocation). Frontend: LOW dialog exceeds banner + disabled Confirm + Cancel -> Failed row OK; Batch Allocations history dialog opens/searches/lists (search '26-27/3001' -> 3 rows); status filter includes 'Awaiting Allocation'; Reports/KPI (13) renders; Master Dispatch / E-Way / Vendor Ack pages load. CRITICAL FRONTEND BUG found: MULTI-batch UI happy path fails because BatchAllocationDialog resets its allocate_qty state every 1.5s poll cycle — the AsnModule poll re-creates a new awaiting_allocation object every tick and BatchAllocationDialog useEffect deps [req] wipes rows back to 0. User can Auto-Allocate & see Confirm enabled, but the very next poll (1.5s later) resets rows to 0 before/at Confirm click, so the /allocation/confirm POST sends zeros and backend returns 400 'Total Allocated (0) must equal ASN Quantity (60)'. Reproduced with UI screenshot showing rows reset to 0 while dialog still open post-confirm click. Backend flow is fine — this is purely a React state bug in BatchAllocationDialog.jsx.

ui_bugs.issue: Dialog resets allocate_qty rows to 0 every 1.5s while user is filling them, because AsnModule poll re-creates a new awaiting_allocation object and useEffect deps [req] wipes rows. Blocks the entire manual multi-batch confirm flow via UI (Auto->Confirm works only if user clicks Confirm within <1.5s window; otherwise backend returns 400 'Total Allocated (0) must equal ASN Quantity').
React hydration warning: <span> cannot be a child of <option> at VendorAckModule.jsx line 180 (dispatch dropdown wraps value in a span due to dynamic renderer). Non-blocking but a console error.

action_items: URGENT: Fix BatchAllocationDialog state reset. Suggested fix (one line): in BatchAllocationDialog.jsx change useEffect(() => {...}, [req]) to depend on a stable id, e.g. useEffect(() => {...}, [req?.record_id, req?.part_number]). Alternatively, in AsnModule.jsx startPoll, only setAllocReq when data.awaiting_allocation?.record_id changes from the current allocReq?.record_id.
Optional: silence React <option><span> hydration warning in VendorAckModule dispatch dropdown (do not nest dynamic-instrumented span inside <option>).
critical_code_review_comments: asn_routes.process_queue uses two module-level dicts (run_state, alloc_state) — single-tenant lock. OK today but blocks any horizontal scaling of the backend pod.
BatchAllocationDialog useEffect dep [req] causes state resets on every 1.5s poll (see bug above).
asn_routes /allocation/cancel body is typed as `dict` (not a Pydantic model) — accepts any shape; consider a strict schema for consistency with confirm.

process_queue swallows exceptions inside the retry loop only for AutomationError; BatchAllocationError is handled correctly (no-retry) but the message check in the frontend hasn't been tested for i18n.
asn_batch_allocations documents include a serialized ObjectId `id` via serialize() — good; but `asn_record_id` / `dispatch_id` are strings from ObjectId conversion which is fine.

success_rate.frontend: 80% — LOW cancel + history + status filter + regression pass; MULTI happy-path UI blocked by state-reset bug

seed_data_creation: Test suites created TEST/BA-MULTI-*, TEST/BA-LOW-*, TEST/NOBATCH-*, TEST/UI-MULTI-*, TEST/UI-LOW-* master_dispatch docs. Cleaned up 8 TEST/ master_dispatch docs post-run. Preserved GEW-MD-BA1 (26-27/3001, ASN26162402 + 3 allocation rows) and GEW-MD-BA2 (26-27/3002). asn_creation records for TEST/ invoices remain (no cascade delete on MD delete) but do not affect app functionality.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend batch allocation flow is fully green (16 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_batch_allocation.py — run with `-n 0` to serialize, since only one ASN run + one pending allocation is allowed globally). CRITICAL frontend bug: BatchAllocationDialog resets rows on every poll (~1.5s). To re-verify after fix: seed a fresh BATCH-MULTI-* master_dispatch, import, edit PO+PDI, click asn-run-{invoice_no}, wait ~10s, then Auto Allocate + Confirm — row should turn Completed. Preserved DB rows: asn_batch_allocations for ASN26162402 (3 rows for 26-27/3001).
rca of the issue: AsnModule.jsx startPoll() does `setAllocReq(data.awaiting_allocation || null)` inside a setInterval every 1500ms. Even though awaiting_allocation contents are stable while the backend waits, JSON.parse creates a new object identity each poll → new React prop → BatchAllocationDialog useEffect(() => {...init...}, [req]) fires → setRows(init) → all user-typed allocate_qty values wiped to auto-init (0 for multi-batch). The user's Auto-Allocate value survives only until the next poll tick. Fix: depend on a stable key (req?.record_id) in the useEffect, OR only update allocReq state when its record_id changes in AsnModule startPoll: `setAllocReq(prev => prev?.record_id === data.awaiting_allocation?.record_id ? prev : (data.awaiting_allocation || null))`.
